commit.message:
Fix column layout: remove spurious +cw/2 offset from col0X

col0X is used as the top-left x of the first card, so it should be
(innerWidth - totalColW) / 2 for correct centering. The extra +cw/2
shifted the entire column layout right by half a card width, causing
the rightmost column to overflow the viewport once card sizes grew
large enough (e.g. on iPad after the scaleToFit changes).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: 40-thieves
repository.description: Classic Solitaire game

commit.message:
feat: otto manages its own copilot headless server via bgtask

Remove the user-facing copilot_server config option. Otto now
auto-starts a copilot headless server via bgtask on port 4321 at
server startup. Both the dashboard and PR monitoring LLM client
connect to this managed server.

Benefits:
- Zero config: works out of the box, no manual server setup
- Version parity: otto starts the user's installed copilot binary,
  ensuring protocol compatibility
- No pkg corruption: one server process, no competing embedded
  binary downloads racing with the user's CLI sessions
- Survives restarts: bgtask with --restart always

Also reverts SDK to released v0.1.30 (stable) since the managed
server approach avoids the protocol v3 mismatch entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Smarter multi-disc handling and album path detection (#265)

## Description

This PR improves the artifacts that Filetote can see and handle for
multi-disc albums.

Due to the nature of [beet's
events](https://beets.readthedocs.io/en/stable/dev/plugins/events.html),
the scanning of artifacts was historically scoped only to the individual
media file's directory. When importing multi-disc albums where tracks
are organized into subdirectories (e.g., `Disc 1/`), Filetote would
detect and handle artifacts inside those subdirectories, but had no way
to grab artifacts in the parent album directory.

In the example below, files like readme.txt and the contents of artwork/
were previously ignored:

```
the_album/
├── readme.txt      <-- Previously ignored, now handled after this PR
├── playlist.m3u    <-- Previously ignored, now handled after this PR
├── booklet.pdf     <-- Previously ignored, now handled after this PR
├── artwork/        <-- Previously ignored, now handled after this PR
│   ├── art1.jpeg
│   ├── art2.jpeg
│   └── art3.jpeg
├── disc 1/
│   ├── track_1.mp3
│   └── artifact1.file
└── disc 2/
    ├── track_1.mp3
    └── artifact2.file
```

With this PR, Filetote now detects when a media item belongs to a
multi-disc album and performs a secondary scan of the **parent album
directory**.

It does this carefully and safely to prevent "cross-contamination":

- Scan the parent folder (for `readme.txt`, `artwork/`, etc.).
- Scan the specific disc folder (for `artifact1.file`).
- Ignores "sibling" disc folders (e.g., when processing `Disc 1`, scan
the parent but skip `Disc 2/` to prevent duplication).

This ensures that album artifacts (like artwork) are captured, while
disc-specific artifacts remain correctly associated with their
respective discs. This also ensures that artifacts in parent directories
(e.g., `Album/01.lrc` relative to `Album/Disc 1/01.mp3`) are explicitly
prevented from pairing to avoid false positives across directory levels.

Fixes https://github.com/gtronset/beets-filetote/discussions/222.

## To Do

- [x] Documentation (update `README.md`)
- [x] Changelog (add an entry to `CHANGELOG.md`)
- [x] Tests

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(executor): extend tool nudge to round<=1, raise threshold to 400 chars

After the round-0 nudge, MiniMax sometimes still writes ~280-350 chars of
prose at round 1 without calling tools. Extend the nudge to fire on rounds
0 and 1 (not just round 0), and raise content threshold from 250 to 400 chars.

Both run() and run_streaming() updated.

commit.message:
Remove resolved ActiveIssue(#14378) skip attributes from IO.FileSystem tests (#125092)

Issue #14378 has been resolved, but 6 test methods across 4 files were
still decorated with
`[ActiveIssue("https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/14378")]`,
causing them to be permanently skipped on all platforms.

## Description

- **`Directory/Delete_MountVolume.cs`** — un-skip `RunTest` (volume
mount deletion scenarios); guarded with
`[ConditionalFact(nameof(IsNtfs))]` where `IsNtfs` checks
`FileSystemDebugInfo.IsCurrentDriveNTFS()`
- **`Directory/ReparsePoints_MountVolume.cs`** — un-skip `runTest`
(`GetDirectories`/`GetFiles` through mount points); guarded with
`[ConditionalFact(nameof(IsNtfs))]` for the same reason
- **`Directory/Exists.cs`** — un-skip `NotReadyDriveAsPath_ReturnsFalse`
and `SubdirectoryOnNotReadyDriveAsPath_ReturnsFalse`; converted to
`[ConditionalFact]` with `throw new SkipTestException(...)` in the test
body so tests are properly reported as skipped (not silently passed)
when no not-ready drive is available
- **`Directory/CreateDirectory.cs`** — un-skip
`NotReadyDriveAsPath_ThrowsDirectoryNotFoundException` and
`SubdirectoryOnNotReadyDriveAsPath_ThrowsIOException`; converted to
`[ConditionalFact]` with `throw new SkipTestException(...)` for the same
reason; fixed CS0121 ambiguity on `ToTheoryData()` caused by adding
`using Microsoft.DotNet.XUnitExtensions;`

The two mount-volume tests use `[ConditionalFact(nameof(IsNtfs))]` where
`IsNtfs` checks only `FileSystemDebugInfo.IsCurrentDriveNTFS()`,
ensuring the tests run only when the current drive is NTFS (required by
the `SetVolumeMountPoint` Win32 API).

The four not-ready-drive tests use plain `[ConditionalFact]` (required
to enable `SkipTestException` to work correctly) together with `throw
new SkipTestException(...)` in the test body when no not-ready drive is
found. The drive is looked up via a local `var drive =
IOServices.GetNotReadyDrive()` call in each test method. Since these
tests are guarded with `[PlatformSpecific(TestPlatforms.Windows)]`, the
P/Invoke into `kernel32.dll` is never reached on non-Windows platforms.
This replaces the old `Console.WriteLine + return` anti-pattern that
caused tests to silently pass with zero assertions.

All tests retain `[PlatformSpecific(TestPlatforms.Windows)]` and will
continue to be skipped on non-Windows platforms.
---
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ViveliDuCh <50237907+ViveliDuCh@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jozkee <16040868+jozkee@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Yasser1728 <241481644+Yasser1728@users.noreply.github.com>

security: validate relocation table size alignment

Add validation to applyRelaTable() to ensure relocation table size
is properly aligned to 24-byte entry size. This prevents silent
truncation when processing malformed ELF files where tableSize is
not a multiple of relaEntSize.

Impact:
- Prevents partial relocation application that could allow code
  execution vulnerabilities
- Adds clear error message for misaligned relocation tables
- Includes comprehensive test coverage for edge cases

Changes:
- loader/loader.go: Add alignment check before processing
- loader/error_test.go: Add TestApplyRelaTableMisaligned
- FINDINGS_QUICK_REFERENCE.md: Mark issue #1 as complete

Complexity impact: applyRelaTable 8.8 → 10.1 (+14.8%)
This is acceptable for security improvement.

Fixes security finding #1 from IMPROVEMENT_FINDINGS.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

on the backend to fail.

feat: script CRUD — Rust commands + UI + tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: LLM client uses shared copilot server to avoid protocol v3 mismatch

The PR monitoring LLM client spawned its own embedded copilot process
which is now protocol v3, but the SDK (v0.1.31) only supports v2.
This caused 'SDK protocol version mismatch' errors that disabled PR
monitoring entirely.

When dashboard.copilot_server is configured, the LLM client now
connects to the same shared server as the dashboard (which is v2
compatible). This fixes the mismatch and avoids spawning an extra
copilot process.

Also adds NewCopilotClientWithServer constructor for shared server
mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update Docker sandboxing design with code review feedback

Address 5 review items:

P0: Cross-platform binary mounting — tools installed in container
    image (Dockerfile.sandbox) instead of mounting host Mach-O binaries
P0: API key passthrough — explicit env var allowlist (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY,

P1: UID/HOME mismatch — non-root flightdeck user in Dockerfile,
    runtime --user flag, git safe.directory workaround
P1: macOS Docker I/O — corrected to 2-5x slower (not 5-10%),
    added VirtioFS/cached/Mutagen mitigations
Minor: Graceful teardown — docker stop before docker rm

Also updated: dependency chain (Phase 4 now parallel with Phase 1),
resolved open questions for UID mapping and API keys.

commit.message:
Fix iPad scaling: raise cap 20->32px, reduce margin 16->8px

The previous 20px cap was hit on iPad in both orientations:
- Portrait (768px wide):  filled only 82% of width
- Landscape (1024px wide): filled only 61% of width

With cap=32 and margin=8:
- iPad portrait:  ~25px -> fills ~99% of width
- iPad landscape: ~32px -> fills ~95% of width
- iPhone stays width-constrained (~12px), unaffected

commit.message:
Backend V2: Clean Architecture Rewrite (#9)

* feat: add backend v2 with clean architecture

Parallel implementation alongside V1 on /api/v2/.
V1 remains untouched on /api/v1/.

Architecture:
- Domain/Application/Infrastructure/Presentation layers per module
- Constructor injection (no singleton imports)
- Typed AppErrors with HTTP status codes
- Zod validation in use cases
- AsyncLocalStorage request-ID correlation in logger

Modules implemented:
- auth: JWT + in-memory session store + one-time SSE tickets
- plants: N+1 query fixed (1 JOIN instead of 101 queries for 50 plants)
- watering: full CRUD with fertilizer types
- substrate: N+1 fixed, upsert components, owner checks
- components: admin-only CRUD, JOIN-based image fetch
- images: Multer + EXIF extraction + Sharp processing pipeline
- sales: 9 scrapers, SSE streaming, concurrency limiter
- more-info: OpenAI streaming + parallel plant link searchers

Database:
- database-v2.sql: full schema with utf8mb4
- 12 performance indexes (fixes full-table scans from V1)
- Consistent use of separate join tables (plant_images,
  substrate_images, component_images) — removes broken
  entity_images reference in MySQLPlantRepository

* feat: implement SSE ticket authentication and improve user data retrieval in auth middleware
refactor: update repository methods to simplify updates and enhance error handling
fix: adjust image repository delete logic to utilize ON DELETE CASCADE
chore: enhance plant routes with optional authentication for public access

* feat: update versioning and enhance JWT handling, including type safety and cookies support

* feat(tests): add comprehensive unit tests for core utilities, auth, plants, sales, and repositories

- Created unit tests for utility functions: formatToDBDate, ensureArray, and filterDuplicatesById.
- Implemented tests for all authentication use cases including registration, login, guest login, logout, profile updates, and deletions.
- Added tests for plant-related use cases covering creation, updates, deletions, and retrievals.
- Developed tests for sales entity and scraping helpers, ensuring correct parsing and URL handling.
- Established tests for MySQL repository implementations to verify SQL generation and data mapping.
- Configured Vitest for the backend test suite, including coverage reporting and path aliases.

* feat(auth): improve JWT secret handling with required environment variable checks

* refactor(server): improve code formatting and dotenv configuration
refactor(images): update image upload middleware to ignore unused response parameter
refactor(exif-parser): change export style for create function

* feat(database): add migration script for InnoDB conversion and performance indexes

* refactor(errors): improve stack trace handling and unify error message formatting

* Add unit tests for Plant, Repository, and Sales modules; configure TypeScript and Vitest

- Implemented unit tests for Plant domain entity and use cases in `plants.test.ts`.
- Added tests for MySQL repository implementations in `repositories.test.ts`.
- Created unit tests for Sale entity and related functions in `sales.test.ts`.
- Introduced TypeScript configuration in `tsconfig.json` for strict type checking and module resolution.
- Configured Vitest in `vitest.config.ts` for testing environment, coverage, and test file patterns.

* refactor(docs): update file paths from src-v2 to src for consistency

* Fix security, config, and build issues flagged in review (#10)

* Initial plan

* Fix review comments: security, config, and build issues

commit.message:
fix: chat stream content not displayed — remove context from content chunks

Content delta chunks included context.data_points, which made the
frontend's first if-branch (context update) catch them instead of the
else-if delta.content branch. The answer accumulator was never updated,
resulting in empty message.content and the question being silently
restored to the input.

Fix: send context only in the initial and final chunks. Content chunks
now contain only delta.content, matching the azure-search-openai-demo
NDJSON streaming protocol the frontend expects.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ops): add APNS env vars to messaging service in docker-compose.prod.yml

The messaging service was missing APNS_ENABLED and related env vars in
its environment: block. It only received them via env_file (app.env),
but app.env doesn't set APNS_ENABLED — so the config default of false
was used, silently disabling push notifications.

Added same APNS environment block as notification service, with
APNS_ENABLED defaulting to true so it works without app.env changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: duplicate Telegram responses from overlapping polls (#19)

* fix: add cron RPC method aliases for menu bar compatibility

The Swift menu bar client calls cron.create, cron.delete, cron.trigger,
cron.pause, cron.resume, cron.get, cron.logs — but the gateway only
registered cron.add, cron.remove, cron.run, cron.enable. This caused
'server error' when creating cron jobs from the menu bar.

Added all aliases so both naming conventions work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: include command field in cron.list response for menu bar

The Swift CronJob model requires a 'command' field but the gateway's
cron list adapter only returned {id, name, schedule, enabled}. The
JSONDecoder silently failed and returned an empty array.

Now includes command (from job.message), agentId, lastRun, nextRun.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: lenient cron job parsing in menu bar — handle null dates

Swift's JSONDecoder was silently failing because:
- lastRun/nextRun are null in JSON but Date? needs a custom strategy
- lastResult field missing entirely from gateway response

Added custom date decoder that handles null/missing dates, plus a
manual JSON fallback parser that constructs CronJob from raw dicts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: cron job detail view with run history in menu bar

Click any cron job → see its run history with status (✅/❌),
timestamp, duration, and output preview. 'Run Now' button triggers
the job and refreshes logs.

Fixes:
- Gateway cron.logs RPC now calls getRunLogs() correctly
- CronService adapter exposes getRunLogs method
- Swift RpcClient manually parses run logs (handles field name
  mismatches: startedAt→timestamp, status→result, etc.)
- CronSettingsView: job rows now tappable → detail view with
  back button, job info, and scrollable run history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: quick actions bar in menu bar panel

Adds a horizontal scrollable row of one-tap action buttons between
the header and chat messages:

- 🧠 Dream — run memory consolidation
- 🌅 Brief — morning briefing
- 📋 Status — show agents, memories, cron status
- 🧠 Memory — list all memories
- 🔄 Update — check for updates
- 📰 HN — top 5 Hacker News stories

Each button sets chatInput and calls sendMessage(), so the response
shows inline in the chat. Makes power features discoverable without
knowing CLI commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent 'tool_call_id did not have response' HTTP 400

Two fixes for the Copilot API 'assistant message with tool_calls
must be followed by tool messages' error:

1. Assistant.ts: wrap executeToolCall in try/catch — always push a
   tool response message even on error. Previously if a tool threw,
   the loop broke and subsequent tool_call_ids had no response.

2. messages.ts: sanitizeMessages now also strips orphaned tool_calls
   from assistant messages (where the tool response is missing).
   Handles both directions: orphan tool → remove, orphan
   tool_calls → strip from assistant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: UpdateAgent version detection — use readFileSync + skip -bar releases

- Import readFileSync properly instead of require('fs') (ESM compat)
- Fetch /releases?per_page=10 instead of /releases/latest to skip
  -bar releases (menu bar app tags were masking the framework version)
- Now correctly reports local:1.9.1, latest:1.9.1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* blog: What Broke When We Shipped Cron Jobs (And What We Learned)

Engineering blog post covering 6 real bugs from today's session:
- Silent cron executor (no logging)
- Swift JSONDecoder try? swallowing decode errors
- RPC method name mismatches (cron.create vs cron.add)
- Tool call loop breaking without sending all responses
- CheckedContinuation double-resume crash on wake
- UpdateAgent version 0.0.0 (require in ESM + /releases/latest)

Theme: every bug was a silent failure. Make failures loud.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram responses from overlapping polls

The polling timer fired every 1 second but getUpdates used a
10-second long-poll timeout. Multiple polls ran concurrently,
all with the same offset, so the same update was processed
multiple times — causing duplicate bot responses.

Added isPolling guard to skip poll if one is already in flight.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: strip |||VOICE||| from Telegram replies + isolate cron conversations

Two fixes:
1. Telegram handler now strips the |||VOICE||| delimiter and only
   sends the formatted part. Previously the raw delimiter and voice
   text were sent as-is to Telegram.

2. Cron jobs using the Assistant agent now get isolated conversation
   keys (cron_{agentId}_{timestamp}) instead of sharing the default
   key. This prevents one failed tool_call from poisoning the
   conversation history and breaking all subsequent cron jobs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add building collision on upgrade  merge + destroy mechanics

When upgrading a building, its growing hitbox is checked for overlap:

Same-type collision  MERGE into Legendary Lv.4 (10x output!)
- Two same-type buildings fuse into one Lv.4
- Massive celebration effects
- Achievement: 'Fusion!' unlocked first time

Different-type collision  DESTROY with 50% refund
- Overlapped building is removed
- Player gets half the building cost back as HC
- Achievement: 'Collateral Damage' unlocked first time

Lv.4 Legendary buildings:
- 10x base output (vs 4.5x at Lv.3)
- Golden pulsing double glow ring
-  star indicators
- 8 orbiting gold energy motes
- 'LEGENDARY' label suffix
- Only achievable via merge, not normal upgrade

Lv.3 buildings now show 'Merge with same type for Lv.4' hint.

Fixes #43

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: warn when MCP servers may not work with shared copilot server

MCP servers from ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json are passed via
SessionConfig.MCPServers, but shared copilot servers (connected
via dashboard.copilot_server) may not honor per-session MCP config,
especially if the server process is old. The embedded SDK mode
(no copilot_server configured) works reliably.

Added a warning log when MCP servers are configured but a shared
server is in use, directing users to remove dashboard.copilot_server
if MCP tools are missing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
